solute.description: This molecule consists of a benzene ring substituted with a linear, fifteen-carbon pentadecyl chain and a single hydroxyl group in the meta (1,3) position. The structure is a long-chain alkylphenol characterized by a massive non-polar hydrophobic tail and a small polar phenolic head. It is a large, amphiphilic molecule capable of acting as both a hydrogen bond donor and acceptor via its hydroxyl group, though its overall physicochemical character is dominated by the lipophilic aliphatic chain. This specific arrangement of a long alkyl chain on a phenol core makes the molecule highly asymmetric and surface-active.

solvent.description: This molecule consists of a single central carbon atom covalently bonded to four chlorine atoms, forming a symmetrical tetrahedral structure. It is a perchlorinated methane derivative where all available valences of the carbon core are occupied by halogen substituents. Despite the high electronegativity of the chlorine atoms, the molecule is nonpolar because its perfect spatial symmetry causes the individual bond dipoles to cancel out. This small, compact molecule lacks hydrogen atoms, precluding any hydrogen-bond donating capacity.
